design_philosophy.core_loop: Two-on-two full court basketball with physical confrontation: score points, steal ball through punching/tackling, navigate court hazards, manage possession and offensive/defensive positioning
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward between scoring legitimately vs. committing aggressive fouls; environmental hazards create unpredictable possession swaps
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Street-level basketball brawler—playground rules where physical dominance and cunning matter as much as shooting skill
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately celebrates physicality and rule-breaking (punching without penalty, flying leap tackles, shorts-pulling embarrassment) as core mechanics rather than aberrations. Ref only polices shot clock, not conduct. Positions basketball as combat sport

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty emerges from opponent AI decision-making and ball physics rather than explicit settings. Genesis version had notorious balancing flaw (95% shot success rate made offense trivial). Player skill in timing punches, positioning for steals, and court hazard avoidance determines match outcome

progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight playable characters with unique characteristics selectable; teams chosen randomly from roster (Chicago, Los Angeles, New York, Denver, Natural High, Brawl State in arcade; customizable by operators)

world_building_techniques: Team branding and character personas (Tyrone featured in Video Power animated segments)
Arcade customization allowing local team names/colors to inject player context

faction_indicators: Home team vs. away team dynamic
danger_signals: Encouraged punching and physical assault as game mechanics
Humiliation mechanics (pulling shorts down during flying leap)
Street-level brawl aesthetic positioning basketball as violent sport
environmental_storytelling: Court hazards (soda cans, candy wrappers) suggest neglected/rowdy playground setting
Physical player animations and knockdown states reinforce combat framing
